I'll bet you've been in a brainstorming meeting when the lead person quickly explains what the challenge is. Before everybody in the room really understands the challenge and what a successful outcome is, people start talking and trying to solve it. If you have been in this situation, you'll recognize that this is not an effective way to start a group discussion. The step back component of the influence process is about clearly defining your case. In any group discussion, it's important that everyone sees the challenge from the same perspective and is aiming for the same outcome. To do this, we recommend that you fill in the same document for any future challenge that we shared with you in the exercise in module 1 labeled Define Your Case, in which, as you'll remember, you answer five important questions. First, what is the situation? Second, who's involved? Third, what do you hope to accomplish? Fourth, what barriers exist to obtaining a positive outcome? And finally, what are some solutions to the problem that you've been considering? By doing this every time, we are confident that it will help in generating more effective and efficient solutions. And we recommend that one person takes the lead before the meeting and explains the case in five minutes or less.
